calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar May-27-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Mary Mix for (Executor) Mike Johnson.

The Court stated that this is an amended first and final account, that there is an issue with the request for substantial extraordinary fees arising out of the litigation, addressed the preemptory challenge of Ms. McMahon indicating that it is not a concern today. Ms. Mix stated that the preemptory challenge is not a concern, addressed the fee concerns with a history of the litigation and that the litigation finally settled. Ms. Mix reported that there have been no objections, that she has been contacted by the mother and sister who believes that the intent was for the property to be sold with proceeds to the mother and that there were certain bequests that went to friends. The court stated that the father has not gotten notice since the initial petition. Ms. Mix stated that there have been no objections, that they were provided for in the will, that Catherine was a creditor, paid for the funeral and wants the mother to be the heir. Ms. Mix further stated that the decedent provided for the executor to receive $5,000.00 per month, that the executor gave himself an initial payout but stopped when told that he could not do that without court approval and that there is $166,580.00 remaining after expenses. The accounting petition was taken under submission.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Allen. (Not Reported)

The Court noted that the Petition for Final Distribution has been filed. Ms. Mix apologized to the Court and explained circumstances. The Court stated that it is prepared not to assess sanctions. Status of the accounting and the Order to Appear were taken off calendar.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy R. McConico. (Not Reported)

PROBATE ACCOUNTING STATUS PETITION
Off Calendar Aug-12-2014 Continued To Sep-02-14 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person.

The Court stated that it is concerned about the Estate, that it has been trying to get a status report since September 2013 and that a notice of unavailability of Counsel is no longer acceptable. Sanctions in the amount of $25.00 per day will commence. An Order to Appear will be issued to the Attorney to appear on September 2, 2014 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 to explain he should not be sanctioned for failure to appear and for failure to file a petition for final distribution. The status hearing is also continued to that date.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy R. McConico. (Not Reported)

PROBATE ACCOUNTING STATUS PETITION
Off Calendar Jul-29-2014 Continued To Aug-12-14 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Dennis Livingston for (executor) Mike Johnson.

Mr. Livingston stated that he is waiting for two bank statements to be able to complete the accounting and will file the petition within one week. The court addressed concerns about the number of continuances since 2013, stated that the court still does not have an accounting, that bond has not been addressed and that the status report has not been adjudicated. Counsel stated that the estate is way over bonded. The court indicated that it has nothing in the file to reflect this. Status on the accounting was continued to August 12, 2014 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. If the accounting is not on file at that time sanctions in the amount of $25.00 per day will be assessed, and the additional bond in the amount of $688,000.00 will need to be filed or an amended inventory and appraisal.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera/Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy A. Allen. (Not Reported)

PROBATE ACCOUNTING STATUS PETITION
Off Calendar Jun-24-2014 Continued To Jul-29-14 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PROBATE ACCOUNTING STATUS PETITION
Off Calendar Jun-17-2014 Continued To Jul-23-14 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Dennis Livingston appeared by telephone for Mike Johnson.

Mr. Livingston stated that he only got part of the Probate Examiner's notes, that notice language was in bold letters in the pleading, that the service list was on the end of the same document, that everybody was served and that he thinks each person got their own envelope. Mr. Livingston also stated that the parties who put in a request for special notice are no longer interested parties, that the bars were sold, and that he will follow up and request withdrawals of the requests for special notice. In addition, Mr. Livingston stated that they are over bonded as of right now, that they only received $150,000.00 on the real estate and that they never had an amount over $1,000,000.00 in value. The Court stated that bond is based on the appraisal, that he needs either an amended inventory or to increase bond, and that the Court still does not have any kind of an accounting. Mr. Livingston stated that he will have a full accounting plus a petition for final distribution filed on or before July 17, 2014, that nobody has challenged the will, that it is a statutory will not a holographic will, that everybody is going to get what is set forth in the will and that most of the jewelry and personal property was left to Mr. Johnson. The Court informed Counsel that the accounting will need to take all three bars into account and that the Probate Referee needs to appraise all of the assets. Mr. Livingston stated that two of the bars were appraised by the Referee. Status on the accounting was continued to July 23, 2014 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for filing of an accounting/petition for final distribution, status on bond and a petition to determine who is to take (said petitions are to be filed by July 17, 2014).

Judge: Mary E.Wiss Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Allen. (Not Reported)

Petition Of Third Party Claimant For Specific Performance Of An Enforceable Buy Sell Stock Transfer Agreement
Off Calendar Oct-20-2010 Continued To Nov-16-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: James French for and with Joseph Cappelletti. Dennis Livingston for Mike Johnson (Executor).

Mr. French stated that mediation starts on November 3, 2010, that no further discovery has been conducted and that he would like to have a trial date set. Mr. Livingston stated that written discovery has been done, that depositions needed to be taken, and that the Estate was asset rich but cash poor. In addition, Mr. Livingston also stated that he was trying to get witnesses to come to San Francisco, and that depositions from Experts and two Clients were needed. The matter was continued to November 16, 2010 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The status hearing set for Novmeber 30, 2010 was advanced to November 16, 2010 for status of mediation and trial setting.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Payne.

Counsel stated that they were ready to go to mediation and that some discovery still needed to be conducted. The matter was continued to October 20, 2010 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status of discovery. The Court signed the order referring the matter to pro bono mediation which is to be completed by November 19, 2010. Status of mediation was set for November 30, 2010 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Payne.

MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Dennis Livingston for Michael Johnson (Executor). James French for and with Joseph Cappelletti (Petitioner).

Mr. Livingston requested a continuance to conduct discovery. Mr. French argued that all Parties knew about the agreement and that he was prepared to proceed with said agreement. Upon returning, Mr. French stated that he would turn over everything they had to Mr. Livingston and that they were trying to engage in informal discovery. The matter was continued to September 1, 2010 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status of discovery and for trial setting.
